Blank 'a' is E (ripe banana). Nafaanra has 3 terms. Rule 3 (E unique) does not apply. No other rule restricts E's word. All existing words in Nafaanra are possible.

Blank 'b' is D (fresh grass). Tucano has 5 terms. Rule 3 requires B(ñiro) and D(b.) to be distinct, so 'b.' cannot be 'ñiro'. Rule 3 also requires E(ewuro) to be unique; if 'b.' were 'ewuro', E would not be unique. Thus 'ewuro' is excluded. The remaining options are valid.

Blank 'c' is C (ripe strawberry). Kalam has 6 terms. Rule 2 requires A(tund), B(mosimb), C(c.) to be distinct, so 'c.' cannot be 'tund' or 'mosimb'. Rule 3 requires E(walin) to be unique, so 'c.' cannot be 'walin'. The remaining options are valid as C does not need to be unique or distinct from D, F, G, H.
